title: 14.-Contract Amendment No. 10789: AMS.NET for Network Hardware, Installation and Professional Support services

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to amend Contract No. 10789 with
AMS.NET, Inc. for network support of hardware, installation and professional services,
for an additional $300,000 for a total not to exceed amount of $1,270,778.14 from July
1, 2017 to June 30, 2026.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding for network hardware, installation and professional services in the amount of
$300,000, is allocated for Fiscal Year (FY) 2025-2026 in the Department of Information
Technology’s IT Cost Allocation funds as outlined below. Future expenditures are subject
to Council approval of the proposed citywide budget and annual appropriations
ordinances.
Hardware and Installation
$210,000 Budget Code: 680-35-363-384-6005-000-472-632130-
(IT Cost Allocation, IT Department, Maintenance)
Professional Services
$90,000 Budget Code: 680-35-363-380-0000-000-412-612990
(IT Cost Allocation, IT Department, Professional Services)
$300,000 Total FY 2025-2026: Hardware, Installation and Professional Services
sections.current_situation:
The City’s current network infrastructure comprises over 150 switches, routers, and
firewalls providing access to network resources across the city’s 42 locations. AMS.NET
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099

Contract Amendment: AMS.Net
CONSENT CALENDAR
May 20, 2025
provides skilled and certified engineers to augment staff in the maintenance of hardware,
software and support of the city’s entire network infrastructure. The following are a few
examples of such high-profile requirements for FY25/26:
 The City needs to upgrade its network hardware infrastructure as it is approaching
its supported lifespan. Newer equipment also has the benefit of improved
performance and enhanced security features, ensuring compatibility with modern
security solutions such as Network Access Control (NAC).
 The Berkeley Fire Department is acquiring a new Fire Administration Building,
which will require new equipment for the IT infrastructure. This infrastructure
equipment will serve as the backbone for connectivity.
 The 1947 Center Street building requires upgrades, as the existing fiber
infrastructure is insufficient to support staff network needs. Upgrading the fiber
within the building will improve access to critical city services, enhancing
connectivity and overall performance.
This request aligns with the City’s strategic goal to “Provide state-of-the-art, well-
maintained infrastructure, amenities, and facilities”.
sections.background:
On July 11, 2017, Council approved a contract with AMS.NET for network equipment,
support and professional services. Since then, AMS.NET has provided excellent technical
expertise to assist staff in maintaining the City’s network infrastructure. AMS.NET offers
Cisco networking communications products (used throughout the city) through a
competitively bid contract with the National Association of State Procurement Officials
(NASPO, formerly the Western States Contracting Alliance, or WSCA).
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
A fast, secure, and reliable network allows for more collaborative work to be accomplished
between City facilities, and provides the backbone infrastructure that supports the City’s
online services, including the increased use of virtual meetings and videoconferencing,
thereby reducing greenhouse gas emissions resulting from travel to and from City
facilities.
sections.rationale:
Staff recommend partnering with AMS.NET based on their 30+ year track record of highly
successful network services for local government agencies and its history of providing
excellent technical expertise and customer service to the City of Berkeley. Network
equipment, hardware and software maintenance from Cisco is purchased and maintained
through resellers and AMS.NET has been providing this support to the City of Berkeley
since 2017.
Page 2
Page 3 of 4
Internal
Contract Amendment: AMS.Net
CONSENT CALENDAR
May 20, 2025
sections.alternatives:
Staff considered performing the work internally, however, these upgrades and projects
require advanced technical expertise and support that AMS.NET can better provide. The
City’s investment in modernized business systems must be supported by an infrastructure
that allows it to operate most efficiently. The combination of AMS.NET’s years of
expertise, coupled with their familiarity with the City’s network lead staff to recommend
continuing the existing support relationship.
CONTACT PERSON
Kevin Fong, Director, Information Technology, 510-981-6541
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 3
Page 4 of 4
Internal
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT AMENDMENT: AMS.NET FOR NETWORK SUPPORT AND
MAINTENANCE
WHEREAS, a reliable network infrastructure is necessary to provide city services to the
Berkeley community; and
WHEREAS, the City Council authorized Contract No. 10789 with AMS.NET on July 11,
2017 (Resolution No. 68,080–N.S.), for network equipment and maintenance; and
WHEREAS, AMS.NET is a reputable company that has provided the City of Berkeley with
excellent technical expertise and customer service since 2017; and
WHEREAS, funding for network hardware, installation and professional services in the
amount of $300,000, is allocated for Fiscal Year (FY) 2025-2026 in the Department of
Information Technology’s IT Cost Allocation; and
WHEREAS, the City’s current network infrastructure comprises over 150 switches,
routers, and firewalls providing access to network resources across the city’s 42 locations;
and
WHEREAS, AMS.NET provides skilled and certified engineers to augment staff in the
maintenance of hardware, software and support of the city’s entire network infrastructure;
and
WHEREAS, the City is engaging in several large-scale infrastructure projects for FY25/26
including upgrading out-of-support hardware, setting up the new Fire Administration
Building and upgrading fiber at 1947 Center Street.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to amend Contract No. 10789 with AMS.NET, Inc. for network
support, maintenance and professional services for an additional $300,000 for a total not
to exceed of $1,270,778.14 from July 1, 2017 to June 30, 2026.
